items.headline: LeBron James Sets New Playoff Record!
items.summary: LeBron James made history by setting an all-time NBA record with 22 series-clinching road wins, leading the Los Angeles Lakers to a 98-78 victory over the Houston Rockets in Game 6.

items.headline: Anthony Davis Trade Rumors Intensify
items.summary: Reports suggest Anthony Davis might not remain with the Washington Wizards long-term, sparking trade speculation with potential landing spots including the Warriors and Bulls.

items.headline: Kevin Durant Out, Trade Talk Swirls Around Rockets Star
items.summary: Kevin Durant remained sidelined for the Rockets' Game 6 loss due to an ankle injury. Speculation about his future in Houston is growing, with some analysts suggesting the team may view him as a 'stepping stone' rather than a long-term piece.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Named All-Star, Impressive Playoff Performance
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija was named an NBA All-Star for 2026 and was a finalist for the Most Improved Player award. He averaged 22.2 points per game in the playoffs for the Portland Trail Blazers, who were eliminated by the San Antonio Spurs 4-1.

items.headline: Lakers Advance to Conference Semifinals!
items.summary: LeBron James led the Lakers past the Rockets in a decisive Game 6.

items.headline: Raptors Force Game 7 in Overtime Thriller!
items.summary: RJ Barrett hit a dramatic game-winning three-pointer in overtime to keep Toronto's playoff hopes alive.

items.headline: Pistons Mount Huge Comeback to Force Game 7!
items.summary: Cade Cunningham's stellar performance helped Detroit erase a 24-point deficit.

items.headline: 76ers Beat Celtics, Series Heads to Game 7!
items.summary: Philadelphia secures a crucial victory to even the series.

items.headline: Timberwolves Eliminate Reigning Champs!
items.summary: Minnesota closes out the Nuggets to advance to the Western Conference Semifinals.

items.headline: Knicks Dominate Hawks, Advance to Semifinals!
items.summary: New York cruises to a blowout win to close out their first-round series.

items.headline: Spurs Eliminate Trail Blazers
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs concluded their series against the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: 76ers vs. Celtics Game 7!
items.summary: The decisive Game 7 between the Philadelphia 76ers and Boston Celtics is set for tonight.

items.headline: LeBron James Dominates Game 6
items.statContext: Led Lakers to series-clinching win vs. HOU.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham Leads Pistons Comeback
items.statContext: Fueled 24-point comeback to force Game 7 vs. ORL.

items.headline: Scottie Barnes' Playmaking Masterclass
items.statContext: Dished out career-high assists in OT win vs. CLE.

items.headline: RJ Barrett's Clutch Heroics
items.statContext: Hit dramatic OT winner to force Game 7 vs. CLE.

items.headline: Evan Mobley's Double-Double Effort
items.statContext: Strong performance for Cavaliers in OT loss vs. TOR.

items.headline: Rui Hachimura's Offensive Spark
items.statContext: Key contributor in Lakers' series-clinching win.

items.headline: Alperen Şengün's Playoff Double-Double
items.statContext: Capped off a statistically dominant year for the Rockets.

items.headline: Luke Kennard Expected to Remain with Lakers
items.summary: After a productive playoff run stepping up due to injuries, Luke Kennard is reportedly expected to stay with the Los Angeles Lakers.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade Rumors Persist
items.summary: Despite still being under contract, trade speculation around Bucks superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo continues to be a major offseason storyline.

items.headline: Embiid Probable, Maxey Available for Game 7
items.summary: Joel Embiid (PHI) is probable for Game 7 against the Celtics following his post-appendectomy surgery recovery, while Tyrese Maxey (PHI) is available.

items.headline: Star Players Out for Season
items.summary: Several key players are out for the season, including Jimmy Butler (torn ACL), Moses Moody (torn patellar tendon), and Donte DiVincenzo (torn Achilles tendon).
